Scope / Plan.name: Threat-model design aids
Scope / Plan.description: Structured tools that guide practitioners in identifying AI-specific threat actors, misuse scenarios, attack paths, and failure modes across models, agents, data pipelines, and integrations.

Scope / Plan.name: LLM / agent attack-surface mapping
Scope / Plan.description: Capabilities that enumerate and visualize exposed prompts, tools, APIs, memory stores, plugins, and decision boundaries where adversarial interaction or exploitation may occur.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Data-poison fuzzing
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Techniques that intentionally inject malformed, adversarial, or misleading training data to evaluate model sensitivity, robustness, and susceptibility to poisoning attacks.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Synthetic insert generation
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Generation of artificial malicious, biased, or edge-case data designed to stress test training and fine-tuning pipelines without using real-world sensitive data.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Malicious model artifacts
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Simulated tampered or compromised model files, weights, or metadata used to assess detection, validation, and integrity controls within AI supply chains.

Development & Experimentation.name: Model vulnerability scanning
Development & Experimentation.description: Automated testing of models for known and emerging weaknesses such as prompt injection, jailbreaks, bias amplification, unsafe completions, and unintended capability exposure.

Development & Experimentation.name: Agent-logic corruption testing
Development & Experimentation.description: Evaluation of agent workflows under adversarial manipulation of reasoning chains, memory, tool outputs, or control logic to uncover unsafe autonomous behaviors.

Test & Evaluation.name: Automated adversarial suites
Test & Evaluation.description: Repeatable collections of adversarial test cases that exercise models and agents across common and novel attack patterns to establish baseline and comparative risk metrics.

Test & Evaluation.name: Prompt-chaining attacks
Test & Evaluation.description: Testing techniques that exploit multi-step prompt dependencies to induce policy bypass, goal hijacking, or unintended behaviors not visible in single-prompt tests.

Test & Evaluation.name: Multi-turn attacks
Test & Evaluation.description: Adversarial testing across extended interactions to identify emergent failures that arise only through sustained dialogue or state accumulation.

Test & Evaluation.name: Protocol attacks (A2A, MCP)
Test & Evaluation.description: Simulation of malicious use or spoofing of agent-to-agent and model communication protocols to test trust boundaries and authentication mechanisms.

Test & Evaluation.name: RAG-poison scenario runners
Test & Evaluation.description: Execution of adversarial scenarios targeting retrieval-augmented generation pipelines by manipulating indexed data or retrieval logic to influence outputs.

Release.name: Supply-chain attack simulation
Release.description: Adversarial testing of model, data, and dependency delivery processes to assess exposure to compromised components, unauthorized changes, or provenance failures.

Deploy.name: Tool-chain / plug-in misuse simulation
Deploy.description: Testing of deployed tools and plugins to identify abuse paths where integrations enable privilege escalation, data exfiltration, or unintended side effects.

Deploy.name: Agent privilege-escalation emulation
Deploy.description: Simulation of scenarios in which agents attempt to exceed intended authority, access restricted tools, or bypass control boundaries in deployed environments.

Deploy.name: Cross-tenant data exposure testing
Deploy.description: Validation that deployed AI systems enforce tenant isolation and prevent unintended data leakage across users or organizational boundaries.

Operate.name: Autonomous red bots
Operate.description: Self-directed adversarial agents that continuously probe deployed AI systems for weaknesses without requiring manual test execution.

Operate.name: Continuous prompt fuzzing
Operate.description: Ongoing generation and mutation of prompts to discover new attack patterns and emergent failure modes in production or production-like systems.

Operate.name: Memory poisoning
Operate.description: Testing of agent memory stores and long-term state mechanisms to evaluate susceptibility to persistent manipulation or corruption.

Monitor.name: Synthetic user & rogue-agent generation
Monitor.description: Creation of simulated malicious users or compromised agents to test detection and monitoring capabilities under realistic operating conditions.

Govern.name: Audit-grade attack-path replay
Govern.description: Capabilities to reproduce adversarial tests and attacks with full context and evidence to support audits, compliance reviews, and post-incident analysis.

Scope / Plan.name: AI asset inventory
Scope / Plan.description: Centralized and continuously updated catalog of AI models, agents, datasets, tools, plugins, and environments used to establish ownership and accountability.

Scope / Plan.name: AI posture dashboards (AI-SPM / AI-TRiSM)
Scope / Plan.description: Dashboards that aggregate AI risk indicators, control coverage, and compliance signals to provide situational awareness across the AI estate.

Scope / Plan.name: Risk-scoring boards
Scope / Plan.description: Mechanisms that quantify AI risks based on likelihood, impact, and control effectiveness to support prioritization and decision-making.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Data lineage & provenance tracking
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Capabilities that trace the origin, transformation, and usage of training and fine-tuning data to support integrity, compliance, and incident response.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: DLP scanning
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Detection of sensitive, personal, or regulated information within AI datasets to reduce privacy and regulatory risk.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Bias–toxicity co-auditing
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Joint assessment of fairness, bias, and harmful content characteristics in datasets and model outputs to prevent systemic harm.

Development & Experimentation.name: SAST / DAST / IAST scanning
Development & Experimentation.description: Application of traditional application security testing methods to AI-related code, services, and infrastructure components.

Development & Experimentation.name: LLM plugin, tool, and infrastructure scanning
Development & Experimentation.description: Evaluation of third-party and internal AI integrations for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and unsafe behaviors.

Test & Evaluation.name: Guardrail conformance testing
Test & Evaluation.description: Validation that deployed guardrails consistently enforce content, safety, and behavioral policies under expected and adversarial conditions.

Test & Evaluation.name: Policy testing & validation
Test & Evaluation.description: Systematic evaluation of policy definitions to ensure they are complete, correctly implemented, and aligned with organizational intent.

Release.name: Secure CI/CD gates
Release.description: Automated enforcement points that prevent AI artifacts from being released unless defined security and risk criteria are satisfied.

Release.name: Signing & provenance validation
Release.description: Verification that released models, code, and data artifacts originate from trusted sources and have not been altered.

Deploy.name: LLM / agent firewall
Deploy.description: Runtime enforcement layer that filters inputs, outputs, and tool usage based on defined policies and risk thresholds.

Deploy.name: Policy management
Deploy.description: Centralized systems for defining, versioning, and deploying AI security and safety policies across environments.

Operate.name: Runtime AI-SPM / AI-WAF
Operate.description: Continuous monitoring and protection capabilities that detect and mitigate attacks against AI systems during operation.

Operate.name: Anomaly & drift detection
Operate.description: Detection of deviations in model behavior, agent actions, or data distributions that may indicate failure or attack.

Operate.name: Trust-boundary alerting
Operate.description: Alerting mechanisms that identify violations of defined trust boundaries between agents, systems, users, and data.

Monitor.name: Posture & metric collection
Monitor.description: Aggregation of operational, security, and risk metrics from deployed AI systems to support analysis and reporting.

Monitor.name: UEBA for AI and agent signals
Monitor.description: Behavior-based analytics applied to AI and agent activity to detect abnormal or malicious patterns.

Govern.name: Policy & compliance orchestration (AI-TRiSM)
Govern.description: Capabilities that align AI operations with regulatory, legal, and internal governance requirements across the lifecycle.

Govern.name: Executive reporting
Govern.description: High-level summaries of AI risk posture, trends, and residual risk designed for leadership and board audiences.

Scope / Plan.name: Import red scenarios
Scope / Plan.description: Ingestion of adversarial test cases and threat scenarios to enable coordinated defensive validation.

Scope / Plan.name: Map red scenarios to blue controls
Scope / Plan.description: Traceability between attacks and defensive measures to identify gaps and measure coverage.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Replay red mutations through blue filters
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Re-execution of known adversarial data patterns to confirm that defensive controls remain effective over time.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Corpus diffing
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Comparison of dataset versions to identify changes that may introduce new risks or regressions.

Development & Experimentation.name: Interactive sandbox
Development & Experimentation.description: Shared environment where red and blue teams collaboratively test attacks and defenses without production risk.

Development & Experimentation.name: Defender signal analysis
Development & Experimentation.description: Analysis of detection outputs generated during adversarial testing to assess signal quality and coverage.

Development & Experimentation.name: Reasoning-trace capture
Development & Experimentation.description: Collection of model or agent reasoning steps to support joint investigation and remediation.

Development & Experimentation.name: Auto-ticketing for failed tests
Development & Experimentation.description: Automatic creation of remediation work items when adversarial tests reveal control failures.

Test & Evaluation.name: One-click purple runs
Test & Evaluation.description: Unified execution of adversarial tests with integrated defensive measurement and reporting.

Test & Evaluation.name: Metrics exporting (blue KPIs)
Test & Evaluation.description: Export of defensive metrics aligned to adversarial outcomes for continuous improvement.

Test & Evaluation.name: Success-threshold analysis
Test & Evaluation.description: Definition and evaluation of acceptable performance and risk thresholds.

Test & Evaluation.name: Hallucination vs misalignment labeling
Test & Evaluation.description: Classification of failures to distinguish factual errors from policy or alignment violations.

Test & Evaluation.name: Continuous Integration hooks
Test & Evaluation.description: Integration of purple testing into continuous integration workflows.

Release.name: Purple pipeline analysis
Release.description: End-to-end assessment of release pipelines to identify residual risks across red and blue perspectives.

Release.name: Release-risk dashboards
Release.description: Visualization of aggregated risks and mitigations at release time.

Release.name: Rollback script generation
Release.description: Automated preparation of rollback procedures to reduce blast radius of failed releases.

Deploy.name: Live traffic chaos simulation
Deploy.description: Controlled disruption of live or mirrored traffic to test resilience and detection under stress.

Deploy.name: Real-time policy shadow mode
Deploy.description: Observation of policy effects without enforcement to assess potential impact.

Deploy.name: Protocol spoofing (MCP / A2A)
Deploy.description: Testing of detection and response to spoofed or malicious inter-agent communications.

Deploy.name: Cost-impact tracking
Deploy.description: Measurement of financial and operational costs associated with attacks and defenses.

Operate.name: Closed-loop purple coaching
Operate.description: Automated feedback mechanisms that use attack results to continuously tune defenses.

Operate.name: Red/blue alert correlation
Operate.description: Linking adversarial actions with defensive alerts to validate coverage and accuracy.

Operate.name: Rule tuning
Operate.description: Refinement of defensive detection and enforcement rules based on observed failures.

Operate.name: Agent behavior baselining
Operate.description: Establishment of normal behavior profiles to improve anomaly detection.

Operate.name: Auto guardrail patching
Operate.description: Automated updates to guardrails in response to discovered weaknesses.

Monitor.name: Purple SIEM lens
Monitor.description: Unified analysis layer that combines adversarial and defensive telemetry.

Monitor.name: Merged telemetry analysis
Monitor.description: Joint reporting that correlates red-team activity with blue-team performance.

Monitor.name: Time-series scoring
Monitor.description: Tracking of risk and control effectiveness trends over time.

Monitor.name: Adaptive hunt packs
Monitor.description: Dynamic threat-hunting strategies informed by recent adversarial findings.

Monitor.name: Model-drift vs threat-drift analysis
Monitor.description: Differentiation between benign model evolution and adversarial manipulation.

Govern.name: Residual risk analysis cycles
Govern.description: Ongoing assessment of remaining risks after mitigation efforts.

Govern.name: Risk simulators
Govern.description: Forward-looking modeling of potential future AI risk scenarios.

Govern.name: Feedback to retraining & IR playbooks
Govern.description: Systematic incorporation of adversarial findings into model improvement and incident response processes.

Scope / Plan.name: Risk taxonomy import/export
Scope / Plan.description: Standardized representation of AI risks to enable interoperability across tools and frameworks.

Scope / Plan.name: Visual data-flow mapping
Scope / Plan.description: Shared visualization of AI system dependencies and trust boundaries.

Scope / Plan.name: Export of tests as stories
Scope / Plan.description: Human-readable representations of tests for communication and governance.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Bias / PII scorecards
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Standardized reporting of sensitive data and bias indicators.

Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.name: Signed data packages
Data Augmentation & Fine-Tuning.description: Cryptographically verifiable datasets used across teams.

Development & Experimentation.name: IDE plugins
Development & Experimentation.description: Developer-integrated tools that surface AI risks early in the lifecycle.

Release & Operate.name: AI-BOM / SBOM diffing
Release & Operate.description: Traceability and change detection across models, data, and code.

Govern.name: Framework mapping
Govern.description: Alignment of capabilities to standards such as NIST AI RMF, OWASP, MITRE, and ISO.

Govern.name: Signed artifact stores
Govern.description: Tamper-evident storage of evidence and artifacts for audit and compliance.
